title: brachyglottis southlandica var. albidula
intro: Brachyglottis southlandica var. albidula, also known as the White Tree Daisy, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, White Tree Daisy is often used as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
